key_insight: Materials Spatial Intelligence (MSI) learns directly from multimodal spatial microstructure data to predict properties while revealing the governing features behind material behavior.
engineering_relevance: The framework can help engineers discover interpretable microstructure–property relationships and guide feature-driven optimization of structural alloys.
method: Encodes high-resolution microstructural and deformation observations into shared latent representations
Preserves spatial relationships across modalities for prediction and interpretation
Uses the learned representation for property trade-off analysis and microstructure optimization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to multimodal spatial observations of structural alloys, showing that MSI identifies key features governing mechanical behavior and property trade-offs.
watch_item: Performance and interpretability may depend on the quality, alignment, and resolution of the multimodal spatial data used for training.
paper_title: Interpretable Material Spatial Intelligence for Discovery of Governing Microstructural Features
